title: Lunara Silver Reel repurpose pack: reframe one product-showcase clip to 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9, AI sizzle cutdown, cleaned founder VO, caption sheet + vectorized wordmark end-card asset
grounding_note: VERBATIM from the Freelancer.com brief "Video Editor for Traya Jewels" (a "growing D2C jewellery brand specializing in handcrafted Sterling Silver jewellery"): the editor must "Edit Instagram Reels and Meta Ads", "Create engaging, fast-paced content optimized for conversions and engagement", "Add transitions, text overlays, music, sound effects, and motion graphics where required", and above all "Repurpose existing content into multiple formats and variations" for "short-form content and social media". FICTIONAL / IP-CLEAN REBRAND: the real "Traya Jewels" is replaced with a fictional D2C sterling-silver brand "Lunara Silver" and a fictional founder persona ("Priya Nair, founder"); no real trademark or proprietary data is used. REASONABLY SCOPED to one runnable connector job: the brief is a recurring role, so it is narrowed to ONE weekly Reel delegation — the client hands off ONE raw vertical product-showcase clip of a silver pendant, a raw phone founder-voiceover take, a slightly-off product hero still, and the flat wordmark PNG; the freelancer REPURPOSES that one clip into the multiple platform formats the brief demands (9:16 Reel/TikTok, 1:1 feed, 16:9 master) plus an AI sizzle cutdown for the paid Meta ad, cleans the founder VO, extracts a caption/scheduling sheet, and preps a graded end-card still + crisp vector wordmark for the closing frame. The brief's "transitions / text overlays / motion graphics" are a Premiere-class timeline finish that NO Adobe connector performs, so those are explicitly flagged as the one manual human-finish step; the automated connector pass delivers the reframes, the sizzle assembly, the VO cleanup, the caption sheet, and the end-card asset kit. No deliverable is invented beyond the "Reels and multiple formats/variations" the brief calls for.
one_line_ask: Take Lunara Silver's one raw vertical pendant-showcase clip, raw founder voiceover, product hero still, and flat wordmark PNG, and repurpose them into a platform pack: 9:16 / 1:1 / 16:9 reframes of the clip (same runtime), an AI sizzle cutdown for the Meta ad, a cleaned founder VO, a caption/scheduling sheet, and a graded end-card still with a vectorized wordmark.
full_brief:
Lunara Silver is a growing D2C brand of handcrafted sterling-silver jewellery. This week's Reel delegation is our new "Moonlit Pendant" launch, and we need the raw footage repurposed into everything our channels eat. I'm uploading four assets: (1) product_reel_moonlit_raw.mp4 — a ~40-second vertical hand-held product-showcase clip of the pendant being lifted, turned in the light, and worn on the neck, with native room audio; (2) founder_vo_raw.wav — a phone-recorded voiceover from our founder Priya Nair ("Every Lunara piece is poured, not printed…") with room hiss and some AC hum; (3) pendant_hero_raw.jpg — a product hero still shot slightly tilted and cool/flat that we want for the closing end-card frame and the ad thumbnail; (4) lunara_wordmark_flat.png — our flat wordmark logo we want razor-sharp on the end card and reusable on future covers.

What I need back:
- The product clip REPURPOSED into three platform formats at the SAME runtime (reframe only, do not trim the story): a 9:16 (1080x1920) version for Instagram Reels & TikTok, a 1:1 (1080x1080) version for the feed, and the original 16:9 (1920x1080) master for YouTube / website.
- ONE fast-paced AI sizzle/highlight cutdown (~12-15s target) of the same footage for the paid Meta ad — pick the strongest hero moments (the light-catch turn, the on-neck reveal).
- The founder voiceover cleaned up (denoise / de-hum), voice only, ready to lay under the cutdowns.
- A short text caption / scheduling summary of what happens in the footage, for our post copy and content calendar.
- A polished, on-brand END-CARD still built from the hero photo — straightened, auto-toned, warmed slightly toward a soft champagne look, pendant popped against a softened background, cropped to a clean 1080x1920 closing-frame — plus our wordmark vectorized to a crisp SVG for the end card and future use.
- Drop everything into one Creative Cloud review board deep-link so I can sign off in one place.

Keep sources in our Creative Cloud folder so tweaks are easy. Heads-up: I know frame-accurate cuts, animated text overlays, hard transitions, motion graphics and beat-synced music are a manual Premiere finish — that's the one step my in-house editor will polish on top; from you I need the automated repurpose pass (the reframes, the sizzle, the VO cleanup, the caption sheet, and the end-card asset kit).

inputs.name: product_reel_moonlit_raw.mp4
inputs.gen_prompt: Hand-held vertical 9:16 phone footage, 1080x1920, ~40 seconds, of a handcrafted sterling-silver 'moonlit' pendant on a fine chain: a hand lifts the pendant off a linen surface, slowly turns it so the polished silver catches soft window light and throws a highlight, a macro push-in on the engraved crescent detail, then a model clasps it on her neckline in soft daylight. Warm minimal jewellery-studio setting, shallow depth of field, gentle real handheld drift, native quiet room ambience audio with faint AC hum, candid documentary product-video look, real metal reflections and micro-scratches, NOT glossy CGI, no on-screen text or captions.
inputs.realism_notes: Genuine phone product-showcase footage: real handheld drift, true silver specular highlights, native audio present so media_enhance_speech / caption summary are meaningful. Vertical source with clear hero moments (light-catch turn, on-neck reveal) so video_create_quick_cut has real engagement peaks and the 1:1 / 16:9 reframes are non-trivial.

inputs.name: founder_vo_raw.wav
inputs.gen_prompt: Warm, sincere female founder voiceover, ~18 seconds: 'Every Lunara piece is poured, not printed. This is the Moonlit pendant — solid sterling silver, hand-finished, made to be worn every single day. Meet the new collection this week.' Friendly, unhurried delivery. Render to WAV, then degrade for realism: add steady room-tone hiss, a low steady AC hum, and light room reverb so it clearly sounds recorded on a phone in a room.
inputs.realism_notes: Intentionally noisy/reverberant (hiss + AC hum + reverb) so media_enhance_speech has a genuine voice-only cleanup job that splits speech from background; NOT a music or loudness task.

inputs.name: pendant_hero_raw.jpg
inputs.gen_prompt: Photoreal product hero still of the same handcrafted sterling-silver moonlit pendant lying on soft linen, shot slightly tilted (horizon off by about 4 degrees), cool/flat white balance, slightly under-saturated, a mildly cluttered soft background of props (a linen fold, a ring dish) behind it. Real silver texture, tiny hand-finishing marks, natural soft shadow, candid catalog-outtake feel, NOT glossy CGI, real grain. 4000x3000.
inputs.realism_notes: Deliberately imperfect (tilt, cool flat WB, busy soft background) so auto_straighten / auto_tone / color_temperature / subject-pop-blur / crop all have real work to do for the end-card still. A clear single subject so image_select_by_prompt gives a meaningful pendant mask.

inputs.name: lunara_wordmark_flat.png
inputs.gen_prompt: Flat minimalist brand wordmark on a transparent background: the word 'LUNARA' in an elegant high-contrast serif with a small crescent-moon glyph over the A, solid single-color (soft charcoal) glyphs, crisp clean edges, high contrast, centered, no gradients, no photographic texture, 2400x700 PNG with alpha.
inputs.realism_notes: Flat, hard-edged, single-color text art so image_vectorize produces clean closed SVG paths; transparent alpha so it reads as the end-card wordmark and reuses on future covers. Text-bearing logo -> gemini-3-pro-image for legible letterforms.

outputs.name: moonlit_reel_9x16.mp4
outputs.spec: Instagram Reels / TikTok reframe of the product-showcase clip to 1080x1920 (9:16), SAME runtime as the source (subject-aware reframe only, no trim). Produced by video_resize [A]; the frame-accurate cuts / text overlays / transitions / motion graphics / beat-sync are the flagged manual Premiere finish.

outputs.name: moonlit_feed_1x1.mp4
outputs.spec: Feed reframe of the same clip to 1080x1080 (1:1), same runtime as source (reframe only, no trim). video_resize [A].

outputs.name: moonlit_master_16x9.mp4
outputs.spec: YouTube / website master reframe of the same clip to 1920x1080 (16:9), same runtime as source (reframe only, no trim). video_resize [A].

outputs.name: moonlit_sizzle_ad.mp4
outputs.spec: AI sizzle / highlight cutdown (~12-15s target) of the product-showcase footage for the paid Meta ad, assembling the strongest hero moments (light-catch turn, on-neck reveal). Produced by video_create_quick_cut [A]; frame-accurate timeline finishing is the flagged manual step.

outputs.name: founder_vo_clean.wav
outputs.spec: Cleaned founder voiceover: isolated speech stem from media_enhance_speech (room hiss, AC hum and reverb suppressed), WAV, voice-only, ready to lay under the cutdowns. Background/reverb stems also returned by the tool.

outputs.name: moonlit_caption_sheet.txt
outputs.spec: Short text caption / scheduling summary of the footage's content (scene beats / transcript summary) from media_summarize, for the post copy and content calendar.

outputs.name: moonlit_endcard_1080x1920.png
outputs.spec: Polished on-brand closing end-card still built from the hero photo: auto-straightened, auto-toned, warmed toward a soft champagne look, pendant popped against a softened (masked Blur-Background preset) background, cropped to a clean 1080x1920 vertical closing frame. PNG, sRGB. Designed to carry the wordmark lockup (that placement is the flagged human-finish step).

outputs.name: lunara_wordmark.svg
outputs.spec: Clean vectorized brand wordmark (SVG) from the flat PNG, razor-sharp at any size, for the end-card lockup and reusable on future covers.

outputs.name: moonlit_review_board
outputs.spec: Creative Cloud / Firefly Board deep-link assembling the finished repurpose pack (a reframed reel, the sizzle, the graded end-card still, the vector wordmark) for one-click founder sign-off.

connector_workflow.note: Initialize a block upload of the client's raw vertical clip product_reel_moonlit_raw.mp4 (media_type video/mp4) into the Lunara Silver Creative Cloud project folder. Returns the transfer document + presigned block URL.
Finalize the clip upload from step 1 into a real CC video assetId — the single source the four video steps all reference.
Reframe the vertical product-showcase clip to a clean full-frame 1080x1920 (9:16) for Reels/TikTok, SAME runtime (subject-aware reframe, no trim). [A] async-widget. FINAL video deliverable.
Reframe the same finalized clip asset to 1080x1080 (1:1) for the feed, same runtime (no trim). [A] async-widget. FINAL video deliverable.
Reframe the same clip asset to 1920x1080 (16:9) as the YouTube/website master, same runtime (no trim). [A] async-widget. FINAL video deliverable.

AI sizzle/highlight assembly from the same finalized clip asset, target ~12-15s, user_prompt for fast-paced hero moments (light-catch turn, on-neck reveal) for the paid Meta ad. [A] async-widget — the product widget polls and notifies; the connector's analog to 'pick the best parts'. Frame-accurate cuts/transitions/motion-graphics/beat-sync remain the flagged manual finish. FINAL video deliverable.
Initialize a block upload of the raw founder voiceover founder_vo_raw.wav (media_type audio/wav) into the project folder for cleanup.
Finalize the voiceover upload from step 7 into a real CC audio assetId.

Clean the founder voiceover from step 8 — isolate the speech stem from room hiss / AC hum / reverb (voice-only, not music/loudness). [A] async-widget. Produces founder_vo_clean.wav. FINAL audio deliverable.

Summarize the product-showcase footage's content into a short text caption / scheduling sheet (scene beats / transcript summary) for the post copy and content calendar. [A] async-widget. Consumes the finalized clip asset from step 2. FINAL data deliverable.
Initialize a block upload of the raw product hero still pendant_hero_raw.jpg (media_type image/jpeg) to start the [C] end-card raster chain.
Finalize the hero-still upload from step 11 into a real CC image asset with a fetchable URL to drive the raster chain.
Level the ~4-degree tilt on the hero product still. Consumes the finalized hero URL from step 12.
Auto-tone the straightened still to fix the flat/under-saturated exposure. Consumes step 13's output.
Warm the cool white balance toward the soft champagne end-card look. Consumes step 14's output.
Select the silver pendant subject by prompt on the warmed still, producing a mask to isolate it from the background. Consumes step 15's image.
Invert the pendant mask to target the background only for the softening step. Consumes step 16's mask against step 15's image.

Apply a masked Adaptive 'Blur Background / Subject Pop' preset (image_apply_preset supports masked Adaptive presets per the capability sheet) fed the inverted background mask (step 17) on the warmed still (step 15) so the pendant separates against a softened backdrop. Deliberately NOT image_apply_lens_blur (which is whole-image / no mask).
Subject-aware crop/resize of the popped still to the exact 1080x1920 vertical closing-frame. Consumes step 18's output. FINAL end-card still deliverable.
Initialize a block upload of the flat wordmark lunara_wordmark_flat.png (media_type image/png) so it can be vectorized for the end-card lockup.
Finalize the wordmark upload from step 20 into a real CC image asset URL.

Vectorize the flat wordmark PNG from step 21 into clean closed SVG paths (lunara_wordmark.svg) for a razor-sharp end-card lockup and future reuse. The end-card + wordmark composite placement is the one human-finish step (the connector does not composite). FINAL vector deliverable.
Pull the graded end-card still (step 19) and the vector wordmark (step 22) back as base64 to visually QC the grade, crop, pendant pop, and that the wordmark reads crisp before handoff.

Assemble the finished repurpose pack — a reframed reel (step 3), the sizzle cutdown (step 6), the graded end-card still (step 19) and the vector wordmark (step 22) — into a Firefly Boards deep-link so the founder previews the whole package in one place. [C] headless. FINAL board deliverable.

connector_workflow.inputs_from: moonlit_reel_9x16, moonlit_feed_1x1, moonlit_master_16x9, moonlit_sizzle_ad, founder_vo_clean, moonlit_caption_sheet, moonlit_endcard_1080x1920, lunara_wordmark_svg
connector_workflow.note: Copy all finals (three reframes, sizzle, cleaned VO, caption sheet, end-card still, vector wordmark) into one dated handoff folder in the client's Creative Cloud so sources + deliverables sit together for easy tweaks (the brief's 'keep sources in CC' ask).

chaining_note: One continuous output-to-input workflow anchored on the four client-supplied assets. The video spine: the raw clip is uploaded (1) then finalized (2 -> reel_asset), and that single finalized assetId is reused by four async steps — video_resize to 9:16 (3), video_resize to 1:1 (4), video_resize to 16:9 (5), video_create_quick_cut to the sizzle (6) — and by media_summarize (10) for the caption sheet. The audio branch chains upload-init (7) -> finalize (8 -> vo_asset) -> media_enhance_speech (9 -> founder_vo_clean). The end-card raster spine is a strict linear-plus-fork [C] chain: hero upload (11) -> finalize (12 -> hero_asset) -> auto_straighten (13) -> auto_tone (14) -> color_temperature (15 -> hero_warm); hero_warm forks — image_select_by_prompt makes pendant_mask (16), image_invert_selection consumes pendant_mask + hero_warm -> bg_mask (17), image_apply_preset consumes hero_warm + bg_mask -> hero_popped (18), crop_and_resize -> moonlit_endcard_1080x1920 (19). The logo branch chains upload-init (20) -> finalize (21 -> logo_asset) -> image_vectorize -> lunara_wordmark_svg (22). asset_inline_preview (23) QCs the end-card (19) + vector (22). create_firefly_board (24) fans in four produced finals (reel 3, sizzle 6, end-card 19, wordmark 22); asset_copy_assets (25) fans in all eight finals into one handoff folder. Every post-load step sets inputs_from to a producing step's output; the only non-chained calls are the four standard upload-init idioms (1,7,11,20), each immediately consumed by its finalize. The repurpose pack emerges entirely from the chain.
difficulty_rationale: Long-horizon flagship: 25 connector calls, 17 distinct Adobe tools, across 3 groups (video/audio [A], asset upload/board/copy [C], image raster [C]) — well past the >=10 calls / >=6 distinct-tool floor. Difficulty comes from repurposing ONE source clip into four honest video outputs (three same-length reframes + an AI sizzle) at the async [A] tier, a real voice-only speech cleanup and a content summary, PLUS a genuine 7-step masked end-card raster spine (straighten -> auto-tone -> temperature -> select-by-prompt -> invert -> masked Subject-Pop/Blur-Background preset -> crop) and a logo vectorize, then two fan-in convergence steps (Firefly board + CC handoff folder). Execution modes are honestly tagged: the four video/audio steps are [A] async-widget (entitled, product-widget-polled, not headless-retrievable), while the raster/asset/vectorize/board/copy steps are [C] headless. It respects every hard limit: video_resize is same-length reframe (no trim), video_create_quick_cut is AI highlight assembly (not frame-accurate timeline editing), and the spec states up front that frame-accurate cuts, text overlays, transitions, motion graphics and beat-synced music are NOT connector-doable and are the flagged manual Premiere finish — so nothing from the not-available list is claimed as a core deliverable. No generative fill / outpaint / object-removal / text-to-image / from-scratch art / upscaling; image_apply_lens_blur is deliberately not used (the background softening is a masked Adaptive Blur-Background/Subject-Pop preset via image_apply_preset, the correct masked path per the capability sheet); image_remove_background / image_fill_area are unused; create_firefly_board only assembles CC assets the chain itself produced.

reverify.banned_capability_check: No banned capability is used. No image_crop_and_resize / reframe-crop / generative fill of new content; no text-to-image or from-scratch art/logo (the wordmark is a supplied flat PNG only vectorized); no upscaling / super-resolution; no object-removal-with-reconstruction / inpainting; no bespoke font/letterform design; no unreachable external account. video_resize (n3-5) is same-runtime reframe (no trim); video_create_quick_cut (n6) is AI highlight assembly, not frame-accurate timeline editing — and frame-accurate cuts, text overlays, transitions, motion graphics and beat-sync are explicitly flagged as the out-of-scope human-finish step, not claimed as connector output. media_enhance_speech (n9) is voice-only cleanup, media_summarize (n10) is inspection/summary only.
reverify.notes: Verified against CONNECTOR_CAPABILITIES_v2.md (lines 42-49): image_apply_preset explicitly supports masked Adaptive 'Subject Pop / Blur Background' presets, and most tonal/FX tools accept a maskURI from select_by_prompt->invert — so the n16 select_by_prompt -> n17 invert_selection -> n18 masked Blur-Background preset background-softening chain is the sanctioned in-bounds path, and image_apply_lens_blur (whole-image, no mask, line 44) is correctly avoided. Exec modes confirmed against reference_adobe_connector_exec_modes.md: the four video/audio steps (n3,n4,n5,n6,n9,n10) are honestly [A] async-widget; image_*/asset_*/image_vectorize/create_firefly_board/asset_copy_assets are [C] headless. image_vectorize (n22) consumes only a supplied flat wordmark PNG (allowed); no glyph/letterform is designed. The end-card + wordmark composite placement and all timeline finishing are named as human-finish steps since the connector does not composite or edit video timelines. create_firefly_board (n24) and asset_copy_assets (n25) only fan in assets the chain already produced. All 25 steps server=='adobe'; no Express-widget tools (search_design/fill_text/animate_design/change_background_color), no Canva, no local step. Counts verified: 25 connector_workflow steps = tool_call_count 25; 17 distinct adobe-server tools = distinct_adobe_tools 17. Slot-match verified: category video_edit, operation_family Motion & Audio (video_resize primary op O3), output_modality video, workflow_nature edit, vertical retail_ecommerce all equal the M06 slot; grounded verbatim in the real 'Video Editor for Traya Jewels' brief with IP-clean fictional Lunara Silver / Priya Nair rebrand.
